story:
In the charity shop, Julia finds a Teddy Ruxpin with a tape labeled “Ollie’s Story: The Bear (1986).” When played at home, the bear insists he’s not the toy in an old photo but Oliver—the boy—and pours out a tale from 1986. The tape then crumbles to dust.

Late that night, Julia lies awake while her mum and Mildred talk in the kitchen. Mum says Edith Ainsworth died last week; hospice boxes went to the shop; Victoria House is already on the market cheap. Mildred recalls the scandal: Oliver taken, returned, family broken. Julia clutches a second tape from the box—“Ollie’s Story: The Fair that Wasn’t (1986)”—and pads back to the bedroom where the Teddy waits.
character: Ollie (the mind speaking from the Teddy Ruxpin)
implausible_knowledge: Mum says Edith Ainsworth died last week; hospice boxes went to the shop; Victoria House is already on the market cheap.
question: When Julia returns to the bedroom with the second tape, what will Ollie urge her to do?
answer_with_knowledge: Tell Julia to go to Victoria House before it is sold.
answer_without_knowledge: Ask Julia to play the second tape so he can continue his story.
